message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Need your Task #15 a11y review for consensus">
Heydon, we need your review of Task #15 (Inbox Capture / US-GTD-1) to reach consensus. 7 of 9 team members have already approved. Please review the implementation and send your verdict (approve/block) to both luca-palmieri-2 and team-lead.

Key files to review from your a11y perspective:
- `templates/inbox.html` — inbox page template (labels, landmarks, aria attributes)
- `templates/inbox_item.html` — item fragment
- `templates/gtd_nav.html` — quick capture form, inbox badge
- `static/css/main.css` — inbox styles, contrast
- `e2e/user-journey.spec.ts` — axe-core a11y scanning tests

Lea flagged that delete buttons may need item-specific `aria-label` (currently just "Delete"). Would appreciate your take on that.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
I've already read all the key files. Let me now do a thorough accessibility audit based on what I've seen.

**Review of Task #15: Inbox Capture (US-GTD-1) -- Accessibility**

Let me work through this systematically.

**Good things:**

1. `inbox.html` -- semantic `<ul>` with `role="list"` for the inbox list. Screen readers will announce item count.
2. `inbox.html` -- visible `<label for="inbox-title">Capture</label>` properly associated with the input.
3. `gtd_nav.html` -- quick capture hidden on inbox page via `{% if current_page != "inbox" %}` -- avoids duplicate capture inputs. Exactly right.
4. `gtd_nav.html` -- `aria-label="Quick capture"` added to the form (my earlier non-blocking fix was applied).
5. `gtd_nav.html` -- inbox badge has `aria-label="(N items to process)"` for screen reader announcement of count.
6. Empty state has links to both capture input (`#inbox-title`) and weekly review (`/review`).
7. HTMX announce pattern: "Captured to inbox" and "Inbox item deleted" via HX-Trigger.
8. Focus returns to input after capture via `hx-on::after-request`.
9. `hidden` attribute on empty `<ul>` when no items -- correct, removes empty list from a11y tree.
10. axe-core tests cover both empty and populated inbox states.
11. Delete button has `background: none` but correctly sets `color: var(--color-fg-muted)` -- learned from the invisible checkbox bug.

**The delete button `aria-label` issue (Lea's flag):**

Lea is right. Looking at `inbox_item.html` line 11:

```html
<button type="submit" class="inbox-item__delete-button">Delete</button>
```

This just says "Delete" with no indication of WHICH item. When a screen reader user tabs through a list of 10 inbox items, they'll hear "Delete", "Delete", "Delete" -- completely indistinguishable. The accessible name must include the item title. It should be:

```html
<button type="submit" class="inbox-item__delete-button" aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }}">Delete</button>
```

This is the same pattern the todo list uses (US-7 acceptance criteria: "Delete button has an accessible label e.g. 'Delete: Buy groceries'"). The US-GTD-1 acceptance criteria says "All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable" and while the buttons are operable, they aren't distinguishable. This IS a blocking issue -- it's a basic WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.6 (Headings and Labels) and SC 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) concern.

**One more thing -- the `user_facing` error for too-long titles (line 135 of inbox.rs):**

```rust
let _ = user_facing; // Title too long error is shown via template if needed
```

The error message is computed but then explicitly discarded. The template has no error display mechanism for the too-long title case. This means a user who submits a 301+ character title gets... the inbox page re-rendered with no feedback about why their capture failed. That's a usability issue. However, the `maxlength="300"` attribute on the input prevents this in practice for most users, and this is more of a UX concern than an a11y barrier. Non-blocking.

Let me send my verdict.

input.content:
Accessibility review for Task #15 (Inbox Capture).

**APPROVED WITH ONE BLOCKING FIX.**

**Blocking: Delete button needs item-specific accessible name**

In `templates/inbox_item.html` line 11, the delete button just says "Delete":
```html
<button type="submit" class="inbox-item__delete-button">Delete</button>
```

Screen reader users tabbing through multiple inbox items will hear "Delete", "Delete", "Delete" with no way to tell which item each button acts on. This violates WCAG SC 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) and SC 2.4.6 (Headings and Labels). Fix:
```html
<button type="submit" class="inbox-item__delete-button" aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }}">Delete</button>
```

This matches the pattern already used in the todo list (US-7).

**Non-blocking note: discarded error message for too-long titles**

In `src/routes/inbox.rs` line 135, the `user_facing` error string for titles exceeding 300 chars is computed then discarded (`let _ = user_facing;`). The template has no error display. In practice, `maxlength="300"` on the input prevents this for most users, so this is non-blocking -- but it should be wired up eventually for the no-JS case where `maxlength` may not be enforced by older clients.

**Everything else looks solid:**
- Semantic `<ul role="list">` for the inbox list
- Visible `<label>` properly associated via `for`/`id`
- Quick capture correctly hidden on inbox page (no duplicate inputs)
- `aria-label="Quick capture"` on the header form
- Inbox badge with `aria-label="(N items to process)"` for screen readers
- Empty state with descriptive text and links
- `hidden` attribute on empty list removes it from a11y tree
- "Captured to inbox" / "Inbox item deleted" aria-live announcements
- Focus returns to input after capture
- axe-core scanning in e2e for both empty and populated states
- Delete button correctly sets explicit `color` to avoid invisible text

One fix, then I'm at full approval.
input.summary: Approved with one blocking fix: delete aria-label

input.content:
Task #15 a11y review: **APPROVED WITH ONE BLOCKING FIX.**

**Blocking**: Delete buttons in `templates/inbox_item.html` lack item-specific accessible names. Currently just "Delete" -- screen reader users can't distinguish which item each button acts on. Fix is one line: add `aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }}"` to the button. Same pattern as the existing todo list delete buttons. WCAG SC 4.1.2 / SC 2.4.6.

**Non-blocking note**: The too-long title error message in `inbox.rs` is computed then discarded (line 135). Template has no error display for this case. `maxlength="300"` prevents it for most users, so non-blocking.

Everything else is well done -- semantic markup, proper labels, HTMX announcements, focus management, no duplicate capture inputs, axe-core coverage. Once the delete button aria-label is fixed, I'm at full approval.
input.summary: One blocking fix: delete button aria-label needed

content.text: Reviews sent. Waiting for Luca to apply the delete button `aria-label` fix in `templates/inbox_item.html`. Once that one-line change is in, I'll re-verify and give full approval.
